body: Good afternoon sir if you give me some more information I can forward your complaint to our resident trooper who is our acting supervisor.

Good evening Ms Olson,

You can email your complaint in writing to the attention of myself, the Somers Police Administrator along with the Somers Resident State Trooper, Supervisors for our Town Police Officers or you can make an appointment to come in and speak with us or contact us by phone.    Please let me know which one is preferable to you.  I will list our contact information below.
 
For confirmation purposes that your complaint is about a Town of Somers Police Officer can you please advise the Officers Name (if known)  and a brief description of your complaint to include the date/ time and nature of your complaint. 


Respectfully,
Kim




Kim Littig  - Somers Police Administrator
Somers Resident Trooper's Office

Hello Jimena,

JPD takes complaints very seriously and there are multiple ways to file a complaint.  You could speak with a supervisor, or file a complaint online.  

If you want to speak with someone, you can do it over the phone or in person.  The main JPD phone number is (907) 586-0600, ask to speak with the shift supervisor.  If you want to come to JPD, our address if 6255 Alaway Avenue.

If you want to file a complaint online, here is the link to the JPD website for commendations/complaints:  https://juneau.org/police/commendation-complaint

If you're not comfortable reporting to JPD, you could also file a complaint with city hall.  Here is the link to their website: https://juneau.org/manager/connect-with-city-hall

I hope this helps,

Deputy Chief Campbell


Juneau Police Department,

Good afternoon Mr. Hansen,

 

You have a few options for filing a citizen complaint with the Avondale Police Department:

 

    You can fill out and print the attached complaint form and mail it back to the Avondale Police Department (address listed below).
    You can come into the Avondale Police Department’s main station and fill out a hard copy complaint form at the records desk (address listed below).
    You can call into the Avondale Police Department non-emergency line (623-333-7001) and request to speak with an on-duty supervisor who can take your complaint over the phone.

 

Please include as much information as possible so that the incident and/or officer can be identified. If you have a police report, the officer’s name, or the date, time and location of occurrence, it will speed things up. Thank you and have a great day!

 

 

Sergeant Ryan Myers #1668

Office of Public Safety – Investigations

City of Avondale

Dear Mr. Schmidt,

Thank you for contacting the Department about your complaint.  The La Verne Police Department takes seriously all complaints regarding the service provided by the Department and the conduct of its members.  The Department will accept and address all complaints of misconduct in accordance with policy and applicable federal, state and local law, municipal and county rules and the requirements of any collective bargaining agreements. It is also the policy of this department to ensure that you can report misconduct without concern for reprisal or retaliation. 

Personnel complaints include any allegation of misconduct or improper job performance that, if true, would constitute a violation of ﻿department﻿ policy or of federal, state or local law, policy or rule. Personnel complaints may be generated internally or by the public.

Inquiries about conduct or performance that, if true, would not violate ﻿department﻿ policy or federal, state or local law, policy or rule may be handled informally by a supervisor and shall not be considered a personnel complaint. Such inquiries generally include clarification regarding policy, procedures or the response to specific incidents by the ﻿Department﻿.

Personnel complaint forms are available in the ﻿police department lobby or can be accessed through the ﻿department﻿ website. (www.lvpd.org)  Personnel complaint forms in languages other than English may also be provided, as determined necessary or practicable.   Once the investigation is completed, the Chief of Police or the authorized designee shall ensure that you are notified of the disposition.

Should you have any questions, please feel free to call me at the Department.  


Cory Leeper, Lieutenant
FSET / HOST / SPECIAL EVENTS / TRAINING
La Verne Police Department

Dear Florencia Martinez, 

The Citrus Heights Police Department welcomes justified complaints concerning the actions of our employees. Every person has an absolute right to file a complaint. Your reluctance to prepare a complaint form shall not impair that right. If you do not wish to complete the form, or sign it, it shall be completed by the Watch Commander with whom the complaint is lodged. It is desirable that you come to the Police Department where your complaint can be received during a personal interview. However, complaints can be made by mail or by telephone, and may be made anonymously.

If the complaint is made by either telephone or mail, a copy of the complaint will be mailed to you if an address is provided. An investigator will contact all witnesses, examine any relevant physical evidence, and gather all information associated with each charge made in the complaint. After completion of the investigation, a disposition for each charge shall be made, based on each alleged act of misconduct.

The final disposition of your complaint will be made by the Chief of Police. Departmental procedure allows thirty (30) days for completion of an investigation into a Civilian’s complaint. An extension may be granted by the Chief of Police, if necessary. You will be notified of the extension and the reasons for that extension. You will be notified by mail of the disposition of the complaint.

Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. The complaint form is attached to this email.

Samantha Lofas
Executive Assistant
Office of the Chief
Citrus Heights Police Department
6315 Fountain Square Dr.
Citrus Heights, CA 95621

Keyshawn,

The Clearwater Police Department allows complaints to be filed in any form the citizen wishes to do so.  You can describe your complaint via email; send a letter to my attention (Chief Slaughter) at 645 Pierce Street, Clearwater, FL 33756;  call the office of professional standards at (727) 562-4300 to verbally make the complaint over the phone; or come in person to the front desk at the police department at 645 Pierce Street where you can provide your complaint verbally in person or write it on a written statement.  We would also take the complaint through a third party (attorney, someone you confide in, etc.). However you feel comfortable providing the complaint, we will be certain to process it.

Regardless of the form or how the complaint is received, the complaint will be assigned a tracking number and be logged into a tracking database.  The complaint would then be assigned to a supervisor to investigate.  The supervisor assigned depends on the nature of the complaint.  All parties would be interviewed and a determination made based on the totality of the evidence available.

Chief Slaughter

The City of Buford Police Department has not existed for over forty-six (46) years.   The Buford Police Department was officially abolished by the City Commission on December 31, 1975.  On January 1, 1976 the Gwinnett County Police Department, the Gwinnett County Sheriff's Office, and the Hall County Sheriff's Office began providing all law enforcement services to the citizens who reside inside the municipal limits of the City of Buford.

Kristen,
        First of all, I'm sorry you had an experience with our department that was contrary to what you expect from our officers.   It really depends on the severity of your complaint, but here are some options....

1.  Come to the police department and asked to speak with a supervisor.  A Sergeant will set down and listen to your complaint and address the issue if it's something that falls under his supervision.  If it requires, he will pass that complaint up the chain of command.

2.  If you want, you can just type you complaint in an email and send it directly to me and I'll address it appropriately.

3.  If you prefer to talk with someone, you can call the police department and ask to talk with a Sergeant, or me directly if it's a complaint that you believes warrants talking with the Chief of Police.

Once we know the severity of the complaint, we will determine how to proceed with the investigation.  Please know that your complaint will be investigated and actions will be taken if it's determined to be necessary.

Respectfully,

Chief Nathan Schmitt

The department takes all complaints against officer’s seriously however the department does not take third party complaints or complaints through email.  All formal complaints must be in writing from the person making the complaint and either notarized or made at the city building so that the complaint can be investigated per the Kentucky Revised Statute 15.520.   This ensures that the correct process is used and that the person making the complaint understands that officer’s may have legal recourse if the complaint contains false information.  Our patrol cars are equipped with cameras that would normally record any officer activity.  Our officer’s also wear body cameras that would normally record an incident as well.   If there is an incident that you believe that the police department should review we do that on a routine bases.  If an officer is rude or in violation of some type of policy or procedure then that is handled within the department.  This could include oral or written reprimand and or additional training in a specific area.  If the complaint is on an officer then the Sergeant usually reviews to issue and if it is on a Sergeant then it is reviewed by me.

Chief Brian Zurborg
Edgewood Police Department

Dear Hunter Schmidt,
 
The St. Anthony Police Department is committed to maintaining integrity, discipline, and a relationship with the community of trust and confidence.
 
If you believe an employee of the St. Anthony Police Department has behaved in an inappropriate manner, you are encouraged to inform the department. Frequently, complaints and concerns can be handled without filing a “formal” complaint. If you would like to make a concern or complaint known to the department without filing a written complaint, you may contact the on-duty police supervisor by:
 
→ Calling 612-782-3350 and requesting to speak to the on-duty police supervisor.
→ Coming to the police department at the address below. If it is outside of normal business hours, you can call 911 and ask to speak to the on-duty supervisor.
 
If you decide you would like to file a written complaint, please complete the form fully and accurately. You can access the form at the link below. When describing the incident, write down a detailed account as you remember it. Whenever possible, include the
location, date, time, and telephone number, and the names and addresses of other known witnesses. If known, give the name(s) of the officer(s) involved. If you do not know the officer’s name(s), use the narrative section to describe the officer(s) in as much detail as you can remember. If necessary, attach additional sheets to this form. It’s also important that you include as much as you remember of any conversation you or others had with the officer(s) or any actions taken by the officer(s). Describe in detail what you feel the officer(s) or any police department employee did that was improper. Before an investigation can begin, state law requires that a signed, written complaint be completed; therefore, sign the form in the area marked, “Citizen Signature.”
 
When you have completed the Complaint form, either bring it to the police department or mail it to:
Complaints Investigator
c/o Chief of Police
St. Anthony Police Department
3301 Silver Lake Road
St. Anthony, MN 55418
 
Upon receipt of your completed form, your complaint will be investigated. Be advised that an investigator may contact you for follow up information to assist in the investigation. Upon completion of the investigation, a “Finding of Fact” will be determined. Please allow four to six weeks for the investigation to be completed. You will be notified by letter with the disposition of your complaint.
 
http://www.savmn.com/DocumentCenter/View/82/Police-Officer-Complaint-Form-PDF
 
Respectfully,
 
	JON MANGSETH
POLICE CHIEF
CITY OF SAINT ANTHONY VILLAGE

Good afternoon, 

 

I received your email regarding a complaint on one of our officers. 

 

Our complaint is as follows: 

 

First, please either call into our communications center (704) 878-3406 or come by the lobby and ask to speak with the on-duty supervisor.  Complaints may be taken over the phone depending on the nature of the complaint.  Complaints of serious nature will require you to formally provide a statement regarding the incident.  Minor issues can typically be resolved without a formal statement.  Most are handled by immediate supervisors, if they see the issue is substantial or you are not satisfied with the outcome then the complaint is forwarded to me for review and investigation.  Patrol supervisors are available 24-7 to speak with.  I encourage you to please contact us if you have concerns about one of our officers.  We take pride in providing professional and courteous service to our citizens.  If you have additional questions please feel free to direct them to me.

 

Thanks,

 

 

Captain Chris Bowen

Statesville Police Department

Professional Standards Division

Hello Mr. Williams,

 

Here is the Lebanon Police Procedure for filing a complaint.  If you need further information, feel free to let me know.

 

Procedure for Filing Compliments or Complaints

Professional Standards

Procedure for filing compliments to or complaints against a Lebanon Police Department employee. The Professional Standards Bureauis operated under the direction of Captain Timothy Cohen who can be reached at 603-448-1212.

Commending a Lebanon Police Department Employee

The best way to commend the actions of a Police Department employee is to write a brief letter describing the incident and the actions you think were exceptional. Information such as the date, time, and location will help identify the employee if you do not know his/her name.

If you choose not to write, you may ask to speak to the employee’s supervisor and make a verbal commendation. Commendations received by the Lebanon Police Department are forwarded to the employee with a copy placed in his/her personnel file.

Although our employees do not expect to be thanked for everything they do, recognition of exceptional services is always welcomed. This kind of feedback helps us know if we are doing a good job.

Filing a Complaint Against a Lebanon Police Department Employee

Complaints will be accepted from any source, whether made in person (36 Poverty Lane, Lebanon), by mail, or over the phone (603-448-1212) and ask to speak with the Officer In Charge. You may also submit a complaint via e-mail to police@LebanonNH.gov. The Professional Standards Bureau, when it becomes aware of complaints or allegations against a department member, may conduct an independent investigation or may refer the complaint.

You may also submit your complaint online utilizing our online complaint form. Simply click on the link or visit LebanonNH.gov/ComplaintForm. If you do not feel comfortable reporting your complaint to the Lebanon Police Department, you may report your complaint directly to the City Manager’s Office by calling 603-448-4220 or emailingmanager@lebanonnh.gov. The City Manager will accept your complaint and take appropriate action.

The average case takes 30 to 90 days to complete. This would depend on the complexity of the case and the availability of witnesses. Once the investigation is concluded, the Chief of Police will notify you by mail of the findings.

The final disposition of complaint cases may be any of the following:

1.      Sustained: The allegation is substantiated.

2.      Unfounded: The allegation is false or not factual.

3.      Exonerated: The incident occurred, but the member/employee acted lawfully, properly, and in accordance with procedure.

4.      Not Sustained: The allegation is not substantiated. No sufficient evidence was uncovered to prove or disprove the allegation.

5.      Misconduct Not Based on Complaint (Sustained): Substantiated misconduct which was not based or alleged in the initial report.

 

 

Tim

 

	

Tim Cohen

Captain

 

Lebanon Police Department

All complaints made against the Department or its employees shall be investigated, including anonymous complaints. Anonymous complaints will be investigated to the degree possible. 

A COMPLAINT  is described as- Information received by the Exeter Police Department alleging an act or omission by one of its employees, which has detrimental effect on the rights and/or sensibilities of an individual or group. The individual or group need not originate the complaint; a complaint may be received from any source. There are two types of complaints our department will entertain which are described as below:

FORMAL COMPLAINT - A complaint received under the above guidelines that are submitted in writing, where the complainant is available for follow-up investigation/interview, and/or the complaint alleges criminal misconduct or similar serious offense.

INFORMAL COMPLAINT - A complaint, as defined above, that is received anonymously, or by an identifiable subject not seeking formal action but supplying the information as an advisory to be used as we see fit.     

PROCEDURE: 

1. Attempt to see or make arrangements to meet with a Sergeant or the Deputy Chief of Police. If the complaint does not concern any individual employee, but instead addresses a particular departmental policy or procedure, ask to see the Chief of Police. (You are urged to call either (603) 772-1212 and ask to speak with the on duty supervisor or (603) 773-6126 which is the Sergeant's office direct line)  

2. If you are filing an informal complaint, you will still be asked to complete a report form which identifies the circumstances, nature, and extent of your complaint. The report form requests specific information which the Department needs in order to make a thorough investigation.

3. Persons who initiate formal written complaints shall receive acknowledgement from the Chief of Police or the Chief’s designee regarding receipt of the complaint.

4. The status of formal investigations shall be communicated to the complainant periodically and at its conclusion. 

5. Investigations shall be completed within thirty (30) days from the time the written complaint is registered. Exceptions shall only be granted by the Chief of Police when extenuating circumstances are present. If the complaint is not resolved to the satisfaction of the complainant, the complainant may request a meeting with the Chief of Police or his designee to discuss the matter. 

6. If the complaint is against the Chief of Police, you can meet directly with the Chief, or file a formal complaint directly with the Exeter Town Manager. You can obtain the procedure for contacting the Town Manager either from the Police Department or the Town Offices.  

I have also provided a copy of Exeter Police Department's Informal Citizen's Complaint Form if you wish to fill it out and email it back to me. You should start with speaking with the on duty supervisor however if you feel more comfortable just emailing me the information that would be sufficient as well.  Hope this helps.

Regards,
-- 
Timothy "Josh" McCain 
Deputy Chief of Police
Exeter Police Department

Good Morning Mrs. Olsen,

Please see the below excerpt from our Internal Affairs Policy;

 V.	ACCEPTING REPORTS ALLEGING OFFICER MISCONDUCT

A.	All department personnel are directed to accept reports of officer misconduct from all persons who wish to file a complaint regardless of the hour or day of the week. Citizens are to be encouraged to submit their complaints in person as soon after the incident as possible. If the complainant cannot file the report in person, a department representative shall visit the individual at their home, place of business or at another location in order to complete the report, if feasible.

B.	Complaints shall be referred to the Internal Affairs Officer if immediately available. If an Internal Affairs officer is not immediately available, all supervisory personnel are directed to accept the report of officer misconduct. If an internal affairs officer and a supervisor are not available, any law enforcement officer shall accept the complaint.

So in short, it is preferred that you make the report in person, but we will accommodate and investigate any report of officer misconduct in almost any format. Valaria Lopez, 

Thank you for your email. Please see our reporting process listed below.

Members of our agency are willing to accept complaints against an officer(s) at any time and are available now. 

Additional Information regarding filing a complaint against a Dover Police Officer can be found on our public website along with a point of contact, Internal affairs policy, and complaint form in both English and Spanish. 
https://www.dover.nj.us/cn/webpage.cfm?tpid=15498 

The members of the Dover Police Department are committed to providing law enforcement services that are fair, effective, and impartially applied.
 It is in the best interests of everyone that your complaint about the performance of an individual officer is resolved fairly and promptly. The Police Department has formal procedures for investigating your complaint. 
These procedures are designed to ensure fairness and protect the rights of both citizens and law enforcement officers: 

1. Reports or Complaints of officer/employee misconduct must be accepted from any person, including anonymous sources, at any time. 

2. Complaints shall be accepted regardless of age, race, ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or immigration status of the complaining party. 

3. Your complaint will be sent to a superior officer or a specially trained internal affairs officer who will conduct a thorough and objective investigation. 

4. You might be asked to help in the investigation by giving a detailed statement about what happened or providing other important information or documents. 

5. All complaints against law enforcement officers are thoroughly investigated. You will be kept informed of the status of the investigation and its ultimate outcome, if requested, and you provide contact information. The exact discipline imposed is confidential, but you will be advised of the ultimate finding, namely : 
          a. Sustained: A preponderance of the evidence shows an officer violated any law; regulation; directive, guideline, policy, or procedure issued by the Attorney General or County Prosecutor; agency protocol; standing operating procedure; rule; or training. 
          b. Unfounded: A preponderance of the evidence shows that the alleged misconduct did not occur. 
          c. Exonerated: A preponderance of the evidence shows the alleged conduct did occur, but did not violate any law; regulation; directive, guideline, policy, or procedure issued by the Attorney General or County Prosecutor; agency protocol; standing operating procedure; rule; or training. 
          d. Not Sustained: The investigation failed to disclose sufficient evidence to clearly prove or disprove the allegation. 

6. If our investigation shows that a crime might have been committed, the county prosecutor will be notified. You might be asked to testify in court. 

7. If our investigation results in an officer being charged with a violation of department rules, you might be asked to testify in a departmental hearing. 

8. If our investigation shows that the complaint is unfounded or that the officer acted properly, the matter will be closed. 

9. Internal affairs investigations are confidential and all disciplinary hearings shall be closed to the public unless the defendant officer requests an open hearing. 

10. You may contact Captain William Newton  973-366-2200 ext 4180 (newton@doverpolicenj.org) and/or Lt. Corona at ext 4182 (corona@doverpolicenj.org) with any additional information or any questions about the case.  


You are encouraged to submit your complaint as soon after the incident as possible. 
If you can not personally appear, a member of the agency may be able to visit you in an effort to complete the report. It is not necessary for you to make a sworn statement to initiate the internal affairs complaint. 

The complaint does not have to be made in person. Complaints over the telephone, email, web-based reporting, and facsimile may also be accepted.

If I can be of any further assistance please do not hesitate to reach out to me. 

An Internal Affairs representative(s) has been Cc'd on this email as well. 

Very respectfully, 


Jonathan E. Delaney, CPM

Chief of Police

Accredited Chief Executive

Dover Police Department

Internal Affairs Capt Christopher Berres cberres@ehpolice.com

Christopher F. Cannizzo
Chief of Police
East Hanover Twp

All complaints of officer misconduct shall be accepted from all persons who wish to file a complaint, regardless of the hour or day of the week. This includes reports from anonymous sources, juveniles, undocumented immigrants, and persons under arrest or in custody. 

During business hours, you may contact our Internal Affairs Commander Captain Jon Burger at 908-204-2500 or 908-766-1122. His email address is jburger@bernardspd.org. Members of the public are encouraged to submit their complaints as soon after the incident as possible. If you cannot personally appear at the police department to file the complaint, a member of this agency from the internal affairs function, will visit your at your home, place of business or other location if necessary to complete the report unless requested not to.  

You may submit your complaint by telephone or by email if you do not wish to be interviewed in person or wish to remain anonymous. Under no circumstances shall it be necessary for you to make a sworn statement to initiate the internal affairs process.

Jake,

 

I received your email question on how to file a complaint. The Monroe City Police Department is committed to maintaining a professional force and department. Everyone should know they have every right to make a complaint and that no retaliation will come as a result of that complaint. The Department will diligently investigate the complaint and implement corrective measures if they are warranted.

 

 

To file a complaint you can;

 

1.    Speak to a supervisor about the issue, in person at the station or on the phone. The non-emergency number is 513.539.9234. The station address is 601 S. Main St. Monroe, Ohio 45050.

2.    Come to the station and pick up a complaint form in the lobby or front vestibule and fill it out and turn it back in.

3.    I have attached a complaint form that you can fill out and email. The address on the form for the station needs to be updated.

4.    Write out the complaint in an E-mail and send it back to this email address.

 

If you have any questions please feel free to call or email me.

 

Brian D. Curlis, CLEE

You can file a complaint through our Professional Standards Unit by coming to the police station at 305 N. O'Connor Rd. and completing a form or you can submit a complaint online. If you have access to the internet, you can use the submission form located here:

https://www.cityofirving.org/FormCenter/Police-Department-22/Professional-Standards-Unit-Form-98

Here's the main page for the Professional Standards Unit:

https://www.cityofirving.org/901/Professional-Standards-Unit

In case you can't access either link, the contact information and FAQs are copied below. The phone numbers for the Professional Standards Unit are also included below.  

Professional Standards Unit
This is your police department and we welcome your comments. We encourage you to let us know about the quality of service you receive from our employees.

PROFESSIONAL STANDARDS UNIT FORM
COMPLIMENTS AND COMMENDATIONS
Commend the actions of employees from this department using the Professional Standards Use Form. Verbal commendations may also be given to any department supervisor.

COMPLAINTS AND CONCERNS
You may also use this form if you believe the conduct of an employee was inappropriate. This will result in an investigation of your complaint. You may also request to speak to a supervisor at any police station, or directly to the Professional Standards Unit.

A NOTE ABOUT COMPLAINTS
While the Irving Police Department encourages complaints or compliments, it is difficult to conduct an investigation and make a determination based on anonymous information. If you wish to make an anonymous complaint, in order to assist investigators with the most detailed information, please call the Professional Standards Unit.

SUGGESTIONS
We also encourage you to send us your suggestions on how we can better serve you.

FAQS
WHAT TIME LIMITS DO I HAVE TO MAKE A COMPLAINT?
In order to conduct a thorough investigation, your complaint should be filed immediately following the incident, or as soon as possible.

WHAT ARE THE POSSIBLE OUTCOMES OF A DISCIPLINARY INVESTIGATION?
1. Unfounded - Evidence does not support the allegation.

2. Exonerated - The allegation is true, but it was lawful and proper.

3. Not Sustained - There is insufficient evidence to prove or disprove the allegation.

4. Sustained - There is sufficient evidence to conclude the allegation occurred.

WHAT HAPPENS IF THE COMPLAINT IS NOT SUSTAINED?
Police officers must be afforded the same rights as citizens. Therefore, complaints must be supported by sufficient evidence. If there is not sufficient evidence to sustain the complaint, the officer is notified of the results and no further action will be taken.

WILL I BE NOTIFIED ON THE STATUS OF MY COMPLAINT?
Yes. You will be notified of the outcome at the conclusion of the investigation. If you have questions during the investigation, you may contact the Professional Standards Lieutenant or Sergeant at (972) 721-3510.

Lieutenant (972) 721-2471
Sergeant (972) 721-3510
Administrative Assistant (972) 721-2555

If you are a local resident, I encourage you to come to the police station at 630 Farley Street in Waxahachie and speak with a supervisor. My name is Matt Davidson and I am the Administrative Sergeant for the La Porte Police Department. We have several ways that one may complain on an officer. Even though there are several avenues to file a complaint, the complaint will either be formal, or informal. An informal complaint would be where someone complains on an officer and just wants the issue investigated and address with no further communication after the initial complaint. A formal complaint is where a person fills out a formal complaint form, it gets notarized, and submits the complaint. It is investigated the same way, the difference is, once the investigation is over, the person filing the complaint is notified of the findings, meaning  they are informed of whether the allegation is; Unfounded, Not Sustained, Exonerated, or Sustained. By law, the person filing the complaint is not told of any disciplinary action taken against an officer, only the findings of the investigation as mentioned earlier.

At your convenience you can come by KPD and let the reception/dispatcher know that you wish to file a formal complaint.  She will direct you to the on-duty supervisor, unless of course your complaint is against the n-duty supervisor.  If that is the case other arrangements will be made.  

Initial information will be gathered from you at the time and forwarded to the Chief of Police who will determine the course of action regarding the complaint.  I have taken the liberty of copying the portion of our complaint policy below. 

Respectfully, 

Ricardo Torres
Chief of Police
Kingsville Police Department



III.	PROCEDURES FOR MAKING A COMPLAINT – Any person, including an employee of the department, shall have the right to file a citizen complaint.

A.	Complaints may originate orally or in writing.

B.	Anonymous complainants may initiate the complaint process when corroborating information is known or when the seriousness of the allegation warrants investigation. 

C.	Formal complaints must be in writing and specifically state the nature of the event and facts in support of the allegation. A statement should be taken, sworn to in front of a notary and signed by the complainant. 

D.	Any person accused in a complaint, formal or informal, shall have the right to respond to the allegations.

E.	If a complaint does not contain sufficient allegations or facts to support it, and cannot be substantiated by investigation, the complaint shall be dismissed as unfounded and that action reported to the employee complained against. 

F.	If it becomes apparent at any stage of the complaint process that criminal activity may be involved by an employee, the Chief of Police may assign an investigator or ask for assistance from a third party law enforcement entity to investigate the incident for presentation to the appropriate prosecuting attorney. 

G.	All persons who file complaints shall be informed, if requested, of the final disposition of their complaints. 

H.	While encouraging the filing of legitimate complaints against employees as a means by which they can be held accountable to the public, the department simultaneously seeks to hold members of the public responsible for the filing of false and malicious allegations against police employees. 

I.	Citizens wishing to make a complaint against any employee of the department are referred to the accused employee’s immediate supervisor, if that supervisor is on-duty. If the accused employee’s immediate supervisor is off-duty, complaints are referred to any available supervisor. Citizens may also file a complaint directly with the Internal Affairs Sergeant. 

J.	If the first-line supervisor or other investigators determine that the complainant is apparently under the influence of an intoxicant or drug, or appears to have a mental disorder, or displays any other trait or condition bearing on his or her credibility, the supervisor or investigator shall note these conditions.  Any visible marks or injuries relative to the allegation shall be noted and photographed.

K.	Prisoners or arrestees also may make complaints. Circumstances may require a department representative meet the complainant at a jail or prison for an interview.  If appropriate, the representative will have photographs taken of prisoners' injuries.

L.	An employee who receives a complaint through U.S. mail shall place the correspondence and envelope in a sealed envelope and forward it to the Chief of Police, who shall determine investigative responsibility.

M.	Complaints received by telephone by dispatchers or other employees shall be courteously and promptly referred to a supervisor or the Chief of Police.  The dispatcher or employee shall record the name and telephone number of the complainant and state that the Chief of Police or, if unavailable, the supervisor will call back as soon as practical.

N.	The above procedure may also be used when department employees desire to enter a complaint against any other employee governed by this order. Internally reported violations are not generally accepted if the alleged incident is older than thirty (30) days unless one of the following conditions exist:

			a.	There is good cause for not making the complaint sooner.

			b.	In a criminal allegation, the statute of limitations will prevail.  


O.	In every case, the Chief of Police will be notified of any complaint as soon as possible by the supervisor receiving the complaint.  Complaints received overnight will be brought to the Chief’s attention the next workday.  Complaints alleging a violation of the law or any serious violation should be reported immediately regardless of the time of day.

Good afternoon Mr. Rodriguez.

I apologies for the delay in my reply.   Yesterday, I was on a day off and just saw your email. 

It is regrettable that you had a negative experience with a Dallas Police Department officer.   We at our department make every effort to give the highest quality service to all of our residents.

Below are the instructions on how to file a complaint or follow this link:  https://dallaspolice.net/division/internalaffairs/complaint

File A Complaint Against an Employee

Note, this portion of the website is only for filing a complaint against an employee(s) of the Dallas Police Department.  If you have a criminal matter to report which does not involve a departmental employee, you may dial *911, use the Dallas Online Reporting System (DORS) to submit an online police report, or consult the Department Directory for a detailed listing of telephone numbers.

On October 1, 2019, the Office of Community Police Oversight Board was given responsibility of providing an accessible process that ensures fair acceptance and processing of external administrative complaints.

The Police Department's goal is to make this process available to all persons who believe they have been aggrieved during an interaction with an employee of the Dallas Police Department.   A formal complaint may be made in one of several ways:

Contact the Office of Community Police Oversight by calling: (214) 671-8283.

A person may come to the Internal Affairs Division, Monday - Friday, 8:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m., on the second floor of the Jack Evans Police Headquarters.  Check-in at the front desk, and ask to speak with an Internal Affairs Detective.  A detective will come get you and help walk you through the entire process.

A person may go to any of the seven police stations around the City of Dallas and ask to speak to a supervisor.  The supervisor will help you initiate the complaint process and provide you with a complaint form to complete.

A person may submit an External Administrative Compliant Form electronically from link below to us or the Office of Community Police Oversight:

PC or Mac users will need to ensure they have Adobe Reader installed on their computer.
If you do not have Adobe Reader you can download free from the below link.
https://get.adobe.com/reader/

Once you have installed the software on your computer click the link for External Administrative Complaint Form and open in Adobe Reader and complete form.
Smartphones and Tablets
If you do not have the Adobe Reader application you will need to download it to your device for free.
The app can be found in both Google Play Store and the Apple App Store for free.

Once you have installed the Application on your smart device click the link for Citizen Complaint Form and open in Adobe Reader and complete the form.
A person may print off an External Administrative Complaint Form available at the link provided below and return it to us in one of several ways:
Mail or deliver it directly to the Office of Community Police Oversight: Dallas City Hall, 1500 Marilla St. 5CS, Dallas TX 75201.
Email it to Office of Community Police Oversight: OCPO@dallascityhall.com

Deliver it in person, e-mail, mail, of fax, to the Dallas Police Department, Internal Affairs Division.

Deliver to any police supervisor at any City of Dallas police facility.
A summary of how to file a complaint and an overview of the process after a complaint has been submitted can be found by reading the How to File A Complaint.pdf or Cómo Presentar Una Queja.pdf informational guide.

Good Morning:

Here is a link that will assist you with your inquiry: https://www.fdl.wi.gov/police/personnel-complaints/. I attached the citizen complaint form to this email. I copied the information below to assist you in understanding the process.   You can start by calling 920-906-5555 and requesting to speak to a supervisor.  

Complaint on Personnel:
The Fond du Lac Police Department understands the importance of having a trusting relationship with our community members.

We know there will be times when members of our community may not agree with the decisions we make, or an employee may not act appropriately.  We encourage citizens to contact us if there are concerns and a supervisor will meet with you.

We take complaints very seriously and see them as opportunities to improve our service, identify employees who may not act appropriately, or educate the public on police procedures and our decision making.

We also know some people may not be comfortable meeting with us in person.  To allow those people the opportunity to voice their concerns we have attached a form that can be completed and dropped off or mailed directly to the Assistant Chief of Police/Administration.

Citizen Complaint Process:
The Fond du Lac Police Department has an obligation to investigate allegations of misconduct to protect the integrity of the agency and its employees and to instill public confidence in the agency.  All complaints of misconduct will be thoroughly investigated by a supervisory member of the department.  To make a complaint, you need only contact the department and explain that you wish to complain about an employee’s conduct.  You may do so by appearing at the department or by forwarding a written account of your complaint to the Assistant Chief of Police/Administration (jlaridaen@fdl.wi.gov). 

Upon receipt of your complaint, an investigation will be initiated.  To facilitate the investigation process, you may be asked to provide a written statement describing the employee’s conduct.  In addition, you may be subpoenaed and required to testify under oath in a civil or criminal proceeding, as the employee involved may be subject to discipline by the Fond du Lac Police Department and/or prosecuted for violations of the law.  Finally, malicious and deliberate false accusations are occasionally made against the agency or its employees.  Knowingly filing a false report of misconduct against a law enforcement officer is a violation of the law and is punishable by a fine of up to $10,000 pursuant to Wisconsin ss946.66

Aaron Goldstein
Chief of Police

Good morning,

 

If you believe a police officer, employed by the City of Platteville (Wisconsin), has violated a department rule, city ordinance, state or federal law or standards of acceptable conduct, you may file a complaint with the Platteville Police Department.  Enclosed is a complaint form that can be used for this purpose.

 

A complaint you file can be based on your personal knowledge of a matter or on information obtained from other sources; you must provide the name and address of each source, or otherwise identify the source of your information, such as a document that has information supporting the complaint.

 

If your complaint is made under oath, in the presence of a notary public, the complaint will be treated as a formal complaint.  If the complaint form is not notarized, it will be treated as an informal complaint.  Informal complaints can also be made in person or by telephone, our contact information for this can be found below my signature. In either case, the Chief of Police, or their designee, will fully investigate your complaint.  You should be aware that under the provisions of section 946.32, Wisconsin Statutes, making a false statement under oath of affirmation may be treated as a felony.

 

If you choose not to use the enclosed form but wish to make the Chief of Police aware of a complaint you have about the actions of a police officer, you may write a letter to the Chief of Police detailing your concerns; please cite the rule, ordinance, law or standard of conduct you believe the individual violated.  However, be advised that the Police and Fire Commission cannot initiate a disciplinary action in the absence of charges filed with the Commission.

 

If you file a complaint with the Police Department, you may ask that your identity, information you provide, or both be kept confidential.  To the extent permitted by state law, the Police Department will respect your request.  However, the Police Department cannot promise unlimited confidentiality; the news media or any person may ask to see a copy of your complaint and related documents; the Police Department may be legally required to honor such a request.

 

Complaints filed with the Chief of Police will be fully investigated and you will be advised of the results of the investigation and its disposition.  If you are not satisfied with the disposition, you may then file charges with the Commission.  If you are filing a complaint against the Chief of Police, you may use this same form and it will be forwarded to the City Manager who will then investigate the complaint and determine the disposition.  The City Manager will advise you of the results of the investigation and its disposition.

 

You will find more information about our department and Police and Fire Commission at:

 

https://www.platteville.org/bc-pfc

 

Please let me know if you have further questions or concerns. Chief McKinley is out of the office today and forwarded your original e-mail to me.

Mr. Martinez,

 

I am the Operations Lieutenant for the Jackson Police Department.  Filing a complaint is easy and delineated in chapter 7 of our policy manual.  The relevant section is listed below (7.6).  The entirety of the policy can be found here.

 

We don’t use a complaint form of any type.  Generally, complaints come in either on the phone or in person.  I usually ask for an email or written version of the complaint for clarity after initial contact.

 

Please let me know how you would like to proceed.  My office phone number is (307) 733-1430 xt 1223.

 

7.6 FILING A COMPLAINT

A. Any citizen who wishes to file a complaint concerning the conduct of a member of the Jackson Police Department shall be courteously and promptly informed of the manner in which this may be done.

 

1. Generally, complainants will be referred to a Supervisor (Sergeant or Corporal) who will attempt to resolve complaints that fall within their scope of authority that are considered minor in nature, and can be resolved through mediation or redirection with the employee. However, any member of the Department, regardless of rank, may accept a complaint in the absence of a supervisor to provide better customer service to the complainant. No complainant shall be requested to lodge their complaint at a later time unless an emergency situation exists.

 

2. Complaints may be received by telephone, letter, Email or in person. Members shall not attempt to dissuade any person from filing a formal complaint against any member of the Department.

 

3. Complaints made by persons who wish to remain anonymous will be accepted but will be forwarded to the Operations Lieutenant or Chief of Police prior to the commencement of any active investigation into the matter to determine if adequate information was provided by the complainant to justify further investigation.

 

4. In all cases, complainants will be encouraged to provide a written statement concerning the complaint, but it is not required to proceed with filing of a complaint.

 

5. Complaints about officer conduct from persons not a party to a police action who have no direct involvement in the alleged incident will be accepted and investigated according to the merits of the complaint. In these cases, the identity of the complainant and witnesses, and the addresses and telephone numbers of each, will be requested. If requested, confidentiality of the complainant will be maintained to the extent consistent with the rights provided to the officer(s) who may be the subject of the complaint.

 

6. Matters which experience and judgment dictate are minor in nature and capable of being resolved through employee counseling, training, or

redirection may be handled and documented at the supervisory level (Sergeant or Corporal) and are considered a “supervisory review” and do not require a more formal investigation.

 

Respectfully,

 

 

	

 

 

 

Russ Ruschill

    Lieutenant
